{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Indian Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd| | |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|bd|pd|= | | | | | |nd|pd| |= | | | | | | | | |= | | |pl|pl| | | | |= | | | | | |nl| | |= |pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl| |rl|= || }} =Indian Defence= ===3...Bg7=== The Bishop moves to its ideal square, bearing down on the central dark squares. Black has so far kept the options open by not committing a central pawn move. White can now take control of e4 ready for occupation or develop in a more passive way, the most common is to play g3 and fianchetto the kingside Bishop. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nf3 Bg7'''
